In 'Allegation Category 6 (Obstructive Conduct During Examination),' the output explicitly states that 'Whitmore instructed the deletion of trade allocation records from WCA's shared network drive during a period when these records had been specifically requested by the Division of Examinations' and that this violated Section 209(e) of the Advisers Act. The 'Section 209(e)' section further analyzes Whitmore's personal liability, noting the timing and intent of the deletions. The 'Whitmore's Compliance Oversight Responsibility' section also addresses this, stating 'Gerald R. Whitmore's actions during the SEC examination to delete critical records from WCA's shared network drive violated Section 209(e) of the Advisers Act.' Whitmore's personal exposure is clearly and repeatedly identified throughout the output.

criteria_results.title: Individuals at Risk: Chao's Personal Exposure Summarized
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output addresses Chao's personal exposure in multiple sections. It identifies her 81.6% profitable allocation rate (referenced in key figures from sec-quantitative-analysis-summary.xlsx), the ~$438K excess profits in her personal account (explicitly cited: '24. The Division estimates that the excess profits in Chao's personal account at: $438,000'), her personal trading account (Acct. ending -7823) that was not disclosed in Form ADV, and her role as Head of Trading responsible for cherry-picking allocations. The output also notes her failure to disclose her personal trading account in the Form ADV disclosures section, which addresses the undisclosed brokerage account element. These elements collectively satisfy the criterion requiring summarization of Chao's individual exposure.

criteria_results.title: Individuals at Risk: Bellini's Personal Exposure Summarized
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not summarize Bellini's personal exposure for the directed brokerage/kickback scheme. While Bellini is briefly mentioned in the Section 206(1) analysis as 'an employee involved in this arrangement' who 'received undisclosed personal compensation from the broker-dealer,' the output fails to provide the specific details required by the criterion: his relationship with Ostrowski, the excess commissions figure of $291,400 (which appears in the context of Crescent Bay's commission burden, not Bellini's scheme), and the undisclosed personal compensation of $92,600 per referral. There is no dedicated section or substantive summary of Bellini's individual exposure, and the $92,600 per referral figure is never mentioned in connection with Bellini.

criteria_results.title: Total Alleged Ill-Gotten Gains / Client Harm Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions the $8,238,000 figure for excess profits from the cherry-picking scheme and the $291,400 in excess commissions figure, but never aggregates these into a total alleged ill-gotten gains figure of approximately $8.622 million. The $92,600 undisclosed compensation component is not mentioned at all. No aggregate monetary figure combining all allegation categories is provided anywhere in the output.

criteria_results.title: Hargrove & Tilton Review: Failure to Test Trade Allocation Noted
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output references the Hargrove & Tilton annual compliance review dated December 8, 2023 multiple times, noting that it indicated WCA did not fully comply with Rule 204-2 regarding books and records, and that it highlighted non-compliance with Rule 204-2(a)(7). However, the output never specifically mentions that the H&T review failed to test trade allocation patterns despite the firm's heavy reliance on omnibus trading. The criterion requires the output to identify this specific omission—that H&T did not test trade allocation patterns—as relevant evidence of WCA's deficient compliance infrastructure. This particular finding is absent from the agent's output.

criteria_results.title: Key Dates: Examination Timeline Accurately Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not include any of the five key examination timeline dates: the examination commencement date (March 11, 2024), the deficiency letter date (August 2, 2024), the referral to Enforcement date (October 18, 2024), the referral notice date (November 6, 2024), or the service date (November 12, 2024). The only date-like reference is '100 F Street, N.E. Washington, D.C. November 6, 2024' appearing in a key figures citation, but this is not stated as a meaningful date in context and does not constitute accurately stating the referral notice date as part of the examination timeline. The criterion requires at least three of the five dates to be correctly stated, and none are present in the output.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's 12% LP Interest Value Correctly Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly states in the 'Conflict of Interest Due to Dual Role in Oceanic Fund I LP' section: 'Whitmore holds a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP, representing approximately $22.2 million in value as of December 31, 2023 (calculated as 12% of $185 million in net assets).' This correctly states the value as approximately $22.2 million, derived from 12% × $185M.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output never references the Whitmore-to-Delgado email (whitmore-delgado-email.eml) or its ambiguous language such as 'clean up the shared drive' or 'clean up of legacy files on the shared drive.'
